site.name: genome
site.tagline: Understand any system without reading its code.
site.description: genome turns one editable nexovia.toml into a self-contained, offline HTML map of your project — its architecture, every module, what each part does and why — so architects, engineers, and executives can see it, question it, and approve it in the browser.

install.label: Install in one line
install.note: Single self-contained binary. No runtime, no dependencies, fully offline. Verifies its own SHA-256.

value.title: See it, don't read it
value.problem: Understanding a system means reading code, chasing authors, or trusting a stale wiki.
value.solution: One offline HTML map of the architecture and every module — switch Exec / Arch / Eng lenses, expand any part for its summary and rationale.

value.title: Question & approve
value.problem: Non-developers can't safely sign off on something they can't inspect.
value.solution: Ask a question or record an approval against any node or the whole snapshot. Every decision is captured in version control, not a chat thread.

value.title: Honest by construction
value.problem: AI-generated docs blur what's known versus guessed, and drift the moment they're written.
value.solution: Every field carries provenance and confidence; machine guesses never overwrite human edits. Re-render and it's never stale.

how.desc: Turn a spec or one-pager into a structured genome of design-first nodes.
Optionally fill plain-language summaries with an embedded model — fully offline, stamped by=llm with a confidence.
Raise questions and record approvals, per node or per snapshot — written back into the TOML.
Generate a self-contained, read-only HTML map. Re-render to refresh; the HTML is a pure projection of the source.

use.title: Review a design before code exists
use.desc: Turn a spec into a reviewable map, then question and approve it.

use.title: Onboard onto an unfamiliar system
use.desc: Read the plain-language summary and why per module instead of spelunking the source.

use.title: Sign-off & governance
use.desc: Executives and architects approve per-module or the whole snapshot, captured in git.

use.title: Keep understanding from drifting
use.desc: Re-render after changes — the map is always a projection of the source of truth.
